seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: output: keep the radius-r coverage goal but make watchtower sites carry per-cell installation costs and require full monitoring of an arbitrary trail graph, so the optimum is a minimum-weight r-dominating / covering-facility-location set (NP-hard) where cost-blind, cost-effective, and local-search heuristics diverge.
title: Wildlife Corridor: Watchtower Coverage on a Habitat Graph
strategies: build-everywhere baseline: a watchtower on every cell (always feasible, calibration point)
cost-blind max-coverage greedy: repeatedly build where it newly monitors the most cells
cost-effective greedy: repeatedly build the tower minimizing cost per newly monitored cell, then prune redundant towers whose cells are all doubly covered
randomized restart local search: shuffled cost-effective greedy over many restarts plus redundancy pruning, keeping the cheapest feasible cover found
